overview: Topological sort yields two phases: the independent Go backend first, then the React frontend that depends on it. This bottom-up approach ensures the API contract is available before the UI is wired up.

phases.name: Backend Foundation
phases.description: Build the independent Task API service that provides all CRUD and filtering endpoints.

components.name: task-api
responsibilities: Expose REST endpoints for task CRUD operations
Support filtering tasks by category and timeframe (today, week, overdue, all)
Persist tasks in a thread-safe in-memory store
Expose /health endpoint and permissive CORS for browser clients
components.architectureContext: Standalone Go HTTP service on port 9090 that serves as the sole backend for the task-web SPA. It has no upstream dependencies and owns the task data model.
keyConsiderations: Use sync.RWMutex around the in-memory map[string]Task for safe concurrent access
Handle OPTIONS preflight and set permissive CORS headers on every response
Timeframe filtering must compute today/week boundaries correctly and exclude completed for 'overdue'
Validate non-empty title on POST/PUT and return proper 400/404 codes
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
  /tasks:
    get:
      parameters:
        - name: category
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: timeframe
          in: query
          schema: { type: string, enum: [today, week, overdue, all] }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskInput' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
  /tasks/{id}:
    parameters:
      - name: id
        in: path
        required: true
        schema: { type: string }
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
        '404': { description: Not found }
    put:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskInput' }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
    delete:
      responses:
        '204': { description: Deleted }
  /tasks/{id}/complete:
    parameters:
      - name: id
        in: path
        required: true
        schema: { type: string }
    post:
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                completed: { type: boolean }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
components:
  schemas:
    Task:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: { type: string }
        title: { type: string }
        category: { type: string, nullable: true }
        dueDate: { type: string, format: date, nullable: true }
        completed: { type: boolean }
        createdAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
    TaskInput:
      type: object
      required: [title]
      properties:
        title: { type: string }
        category: { type: string, nullable: true }
        dueDate: { type: string, format: date, nullable: true }
        completed: { type: boolean }

phases.name: Frontend SPA
phases.description: Build the React SPA once the backend API is available for integration.

components.name: task-web
responsibilities: Provide UI for creating, editing, completing, and deleting tasks
Display tasks with sorting/grouping by due date and de-emphasize completed
Filter tasks by category and timeframe
Call the task-api backend using runtime-configurable base URL
Serve SPA via nginx with /health endpoint
components.architectureContext: React+TypeScript SPA served by nginx. It is the sole consumer of task-api, calling its REST endpoints over HTTPS/JSON from the browser.
keyConsiderations: Runtime config via window.__ENV__.API_URL injected from TASK_API_URL env var at container start
nginx must fallback to index.html for SPA routing and expose /health returning 200
Use fetch with proper error handling and optimistic UI for completion toggles
Multi-stage Docker: node:20 build → nginx:alpine serving port 8080
Rely on backend CORS; ensure Content-Type: application/json on mutating requests
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task Web
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
